summary: Fix input-stream-p/output-stream-p to return NIL (not error) for the wrong direction of a Gray stream, and make make-two-way-stream accept Gray streams with read/write delegating through the Gray generic-dispatch path.

items.title: input-stream-p / output-stream-p: return NIL for wrong-direction Gray streams
items.description:
BUG: (output-stream-p gray-input-stream) and (input-stream-p gray-output-stream) signal "argument is not a stream" instead of returning NIL. Reproduced on the host build: trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp line 80 currently FAILS with "OUTPUT-STREAM-P: argument is not a stream".

ROOT CAUSE (Lisp only): lib/gray-streams.lisp redefines INPUT-STREAM-P and OUTPUT-STREAM-P as generic functions (in the (in-package "COMMON-LISP") let-block, lines 306-317). They have a method on the matching fundamental class returning T, and a fallback method on (stream t) that calls the captured C builtin orig-input-stream-p / orig-output-stream-p. Those C builtins (bi_input_stream_p / bi_output_stream_p in src/core/builtins_stream.c:97-118) cl_error when the argument is not a native CL_STREAM_P stream. A Gray stream of the WRONG direction (e.g. a fundamental-output-stream passed to input-stream-p) does not match the more-specific method, falls to the (t) method, and the C builtin errors instead of returning NIL.

FIX (lib/gray-streams.lisp):
  - Change the input-stream-p (stream t) fallback method (currently `(funcall orig-input-stream-p stream)`, ~line 309-310) to: `(if (%gray-stream-p stream) nil (funcall orig-input-stream-p stream))`.
  - Change the output-stream-p (stream t) fallback method (~line 316-317) to: `(if (%gray-stream-p stream) nil (funcall orig-output-stream-p stream))`.
  - Rationale: a Gray stream that reaches the (t) method is by construction a fundamental-stream that did NOT match the more-specific fundamental-input-stream / fundamental-output-stream method, so the correct directional answer is NIL. %gray-stream-p is already defined earlier in the same let-block (line 285). Native streams are unaffected (still routed to the C builtin, which already returns NIL/T correctly by direction).

TESTS:
  - trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp: the existing check at line 80 `(check "output-stream-p on gray input instance" nil (output-stream-p gi))` now passes. ADD the symmetric assertions: for the gray input instance gi assert (input-stream-p gi) => T; define a gray OUTPUT instance (or reuse test-gray-out-stream / test-gray-cap-stream) and assert (input-stream-p <gray-out>) => NIL and (output-stream-p <gray-out>) => T. Also fix the failure-exit on line 144: replace `(ext:quit 1)` with `(quit 1)` (QUIT/EXIT are interned in COMMON-LISP-USER per src/core/builtins.c:1444-1450; EXT has no QUIT export, so the current call itself errors and masks CI failure signalling).
  - tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp: in the gray-streams section (~lines 4691-4697) the existing `(output-stream-p gi)` => NIL check now passes; ADD `(check "gray input input-stream-p" t (input-stream-p gi))`, and for a gray output instance add `(input-stream-p <gray-out>)` => NIL and `(output-stream-p <gray-out>)` => T.

VERIFY: `make test` passes; `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp` reports ALL TESTS PASSED (the FASL cache recompiles gray-streams.lisp on load because the source mtime changes; if a stale fasl is served, remove ~/.cache/common-lisp before re-running).

items.title: make-two-way-stream accepts Gray streams; two-way read/write delegate to Gray dispatch
items.description:
BUG: (make-two-way-stream gray-in gray-out) signals "MAKE-TWO-WAY-STREAM: first argument is not a stream" even though streamp / input-stream-p / output-stream-p are all T for the Gray args (reproduced on host). The native two-way stream is correct for native string streams (verified by the 0005 tests); it just rejects Gray streams because bi_make_two_way_stream uses a native-only CL_STREAM_P tag test, and even if constructed, raw C two-way I/O cannot reach a Gray child.

PART A — accept Gray streams (src/core/builtins_stream.c):
  - Add two lazily-cached static CL_Obj symbol caches near the KW_* statics (init CL_NIL): GRAY_FUND_INPUT_SYM, GRAY_FUND_OUTPUT_SYM.
  - Add a helper that resolves GRAY::<class-name> on first use, mirroring the STREAM case in src/core/builtins_type.c:181-209: `cl_find_package("GRAY",4)` then `cl_package_find_symbol(name,len,gray_pkg)`, cache into the static, guarded by cl_tables_wrlock()/cl_tables_rwunlock() (same locking the typep code uses). Returns CL_NIL if the GRAY package/class is absent (core build without lib/gray-streams.lisp) so behavior is unchanged there.
  - Add `obj_is_gray_subclass(CL_Obj obj, CL_Obj class_sym)`: if `CL_STRUCT_P(obj)` and class_sym != CL_NIL, return `cl_clos_type_matches(((CL_Struct*)CL_OBJ_TO_PTR(obj))->type_desc, class_sym)` (extern-declare `int cl_clos_type_matches(CL_Obj,CL_Obj);` locally, exactly as builtins_type.c does at line 187). Else 0.
  - Add `is_input_stream_designator(o)` = `(CL_STREAM_P(o) && (((CL_Stream*)CL_OBJ_TO_PTR(o))->direction & CL_STREAM_INPUT)) || obj_is_gray_subclass(o, <FUNDAMENTAL-INPUT-STREAM, len 24>)` and `is_output_stream_designator(o)` analogously with CL_STREAM_OUTPUT and FUNDAMENTAL-OUTPUT-STREAM (len 25). (CL_STREAM_IO == 3 == INPUT|OUTPUT, so the bitwise & correctly accepts IO streams — same as the existing code.)
  - Rewrite bi_make_two_way_stream (src/core/builtins_stream.c:545-560): replace the CL_STREAM_P + native-direction checks with `if (!is_input_stream_designator(args[0])) cl_error(CL_ERR_TYPE, "MAKE-TWO-WAY-STREAM: first argument is not an input stream"); if (!is_output_stream_designator(args[1])) cl_error(CL_ERR_TYPE, "MAKE-TWO-WAY-STREAM: second argument is not an output stream");` then `return cl_make_two_way_stream(args[0], args[1]);`. No GC protection needed (args[] are rooted; the helpers do not allocate). cl_make_two_way_stream stores the (possibly Gray) children in string_buf/element_type unchanged, so typep 'two-way-stream and the two-way-stream-input/output-stream accessors keep working natively and return the Gray children.
  - In cl_builtins_stream_init (~line 1349-1451) add `cl_gc_register_root(&GRAY_FUND_INPUT_SYM);` and `cl_gc_register_root(&GRAY_FUND_OUTPUT_SYM);` next to the other root registrations — the moving GC must forward these cached symbol offsets (builtins_type.c:2169 does the same for its cached stream symbol).
  - Verify includes: CL_STRUCT_P / CL_Struct / ->type_desc, cl_find_package & cl_package_find_symbol (package.h already included at line 15), cl_gc_register_root (mem.h, included), and the cl_tables_wrlock/cl_tables_rwunlock declarations — add the header builtins_type.c uses for those if not already pulled in.

PART B — make raw C two-way I/O Gray-safe (src/core/stream.c, resolve_stream, lines 333-353):
  - In the SYNONYM and TWO-WAY child-following branches, change the child guard `if (CL_NULL_P(stream) || !CL_HEAP_P(stream)) return CL_NIL;` to use `!CL_STREAM_P(stream)` instead of `!CL_HEAP_P(stream)`. A Gray child is CL_STRUCT_P (heap) but NOT CL_STREAM_P, so this returns CL_NIL and the C I/O functions safely degrade to EOF/no-op instead of mis-reading the CLOS struct as a CL_Stream (memory corruption / UB). Native children are unaffected (still CL_STREAM_P). The top-of-function guard is already `!CL_STREAM_P`; this just makes the loop consistent. The real Gray delegation happens in Part C via the Lisp wrappers.

PART C — delegate two-way read/write to Gray dispatch (lib/gray-streams.lisp, %resolve-input-stream / %resolve-output-stream, lines 290-298):
  - After computing the candidate stream s (null->*standard-input*/*standard-output*, t->*terminal-io*, else stream), unwrap a NATIVE two-way stream to its directional child so the existing %gray-stream-p dispatch in every I/O wrapper reaches a Gray child:
      %resolve-input-stream: return `(if (typep s 'two-way-stream) (two-way-stream-input-stream s) s)`
      %resolve-output-stream: return `(if (typep s 'two-way-stream) (two-way-stream-output-stream s) s)`
  - Effect: a native two-way stream wrapping a Gray child resolves to the Gray child -> %gray-stream-p true -> routes to gray:stream-read-char / gray:stream-write-char / etc. (the same wrapper dispatch path the 0001 printer fix uses). A two-way stream wrapping a native child resolves to the native child -> orig-* (behaviorally equivalent to what C resolve_stream did). Single-level unwrap suffices (two-way children are leaf streams). Because every wrapped I/O fn (read-char, write-char, peek-char, unread-char, read-line, write-string, write-line, terpri, fresh-line, finish/force/clear-output, princ, prin1, print, write, format, pprint, listen, clear-input) funnels through these two helpers, this single change covers them all. NOTE: the pure-C unit harness (tests/test_stream.c) never loads gray-streams.lisp, so native-native two-way streams there still resolve in C — unaffected.

TESTS:
  - tests/test_stream.c: add a TEST asserting make-two-way-stream still rejects a genuinely-non-stream first arg, e.g. (handler-case (make-two-way-stream 42 (make-string-output-stream)) (error () :type-error)) => :type-error, and a wrong-direction native second arg, e.g. arg1 = (make-string-input-stream "") => :type-error. Register it in the runner block near line 2663. (Existing native two-way happy-path tests at 2491-2605 must still pass unchanged.)
  - trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp: add a section using a Gray input class (stream-read-char pops queued chars, returns :eof when empty) and a Gray capturing output class (reuse test-gray-cap-stream). Assert, for the both-Gray two-way stream tw = (make-two-way-stream gin gcap): (streamp tw) T, (typep tw 'two-way-stream) T, (typep tw 'stream) T, (input-stream-p tw) T, (output-stream-p tw) T, (eq (two-way-stream-input-stream tw) gin) T, (eq (two-way-stream-output-stream tw) gcap) T; reading consumes the Gray input's chars in order ((read-char tw) ...); writing ((write-char #\X tw) / (write-string "hi" tw)) accumulates on gcap (verify via gray-cap-flush). Add MIXED cases: native input + Gray output, and Gray input + native output, each round-tripping (read from input child, write to output child) correctly.
  - tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp: mirror the both-Gray and mixed two-way assertions with `check` inside the gray-streams section (after ~line 4720, where the Gray classes/instances are in scope).

VERIFY: `make test` passes; `./build/host/clamiga --heap 24M --load trunk/test-gray-streams.lisp` reports ALL TESTS PASSED. (If the gray-streams fasl is stale, remove ~/.cache/common-lisp before re-running.)
